36.reason: The loop condition should be i < strlen(b) instead of i <= strlen(b), because b is a null-terminated string and strlen(b) returns the length excluding the null terminator. Using <= will print the null character, causing extra output like "9�e-1" instead of "9e-1".

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition should be i < strlen(b) instead of i <= strlen(b), because b is a null-terminated string and strlen(b) returns the length excluding the null terminator. Using <= will print the null character, causing extra output like "9�e-1" instead of "9e-1".
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition uses <=, which causes an extra character (likely a null terminator or garbage) to be printed after the last valid digit, resulting in the "9�" output instead of just "9".
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Loop condition should be i<strlen(b) to avoid printing the null terminator b[strlen(b)], which causes an extra character in output.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition `i<=strlen(b)` causes it to access `b[strlen(b)]`, which is the null terminator `\0`. This results in printing an extra invisible character or garbage after the significant digits, causing the output `9\0e-1` instead of `9e-1`. It should be `i<strlen(b)`.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The loop condition `i<=strlen(b)` causes it to access `b[strlen(b)]`, which is the null terminator `\0`. This results in printing an extra invisible character or garbage after the significant digits, causing the output `9\0e-1` instead of `9e-1`. It should be `i<strlen(b)`.

46.reason: [Exponent calculation is incorrect for numbers with leading zeros. Should be -n instead of 1-n. For example, input "0.9" gives n=1, exponent should be -1, but this prints 0.]
